sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The ChildrenKinematics class, implemented in Cython, is a specialized selection template within the AnalysisG framework, designed for detailed analysis of the kinematic properties of particles originating from top quark decays in Monte Carlo 16 (MC16) events. It extends the generic SelectionTemplate class, providing tailored functionalities for handling various kinematic observables, particle identification (PDG ID), and decay modes. This class facilitates a high-performance extraction and organization of children kinematics data for further physics analysis.

sect1.title: Helper Functions (C-style)
sect1.para: These functions are implemented as C-style functions in Cython for performance optimization and direct interaction with C++ types.

listitem.para:
cdef str pdgid(int ipt): Converts a PDG (Particle Data Group) ID to a symbolic string representation, often used for display in plots or tables.
ipt
The integer PDG ID of a particle.
A string representation of the particle (e.g., "d", "u", "e", "$\nu_{e}$", "g", "$\gamma$", etc.).

cdef void merge(vector[float]* ix, vector[float]* iy): Merges the contents of iy (source vector) into ix (destination vector) by appending elements.
ix
A pointer to the destination C++ std::vector<float>.
iy
A pointer to the source C++ std::vector<float>.

def res(ck, data): Processes kinematic data for "resonant" particles. It extracts the numerical values from data[1] and merges them into ck.r_data keyed by a derived name from data[0].
ck
An instance of ChildrenKinematics.
data
A tuple (name_string, vector_of_floats) containing the data.

def spc(ck, data): Processes kinematic data for "spectator" particles. Similar to res, but merges into ck.s_data.
ck
An instance of ChildrenKinematics.
data
A tuple (name_string, vector_of_floats) containing the data.

def rdcy(ck, data): Processes kinematic data for "resonant decay" particles. Similar to res, but merges into ck.r_decay. Handles "frc" prefix for fractional kinematics.
ck
An instance of ChildrenKinematics.
data
A tuple (name_string, vector_of_floats) containing the data.

def sdcy(ck, data): Processes kinematic data for "spectator decay" particles. Similar to spc, but merges into ck.s_decay. Handles "frc" prefix for fractional kinematics.
ck
An instance of ChildrenKinematics.
data
A tuple (name_string, vector_of_floats) containing the data.

def tpm(ck, data): Processes kinematic data related to "top permutation" matching. It extracts values and merges them into ck.top_pem.
ck
An instance of ChildrenKinematics.
data
A tuple (name_string, vector_of_floats) containing the data.

sect1.title: The ChildrenKinematics Class
sect1.para: ChildrenKinematics is a cdef class that inherits from SelectionTemplate. It is tailored for collecting and organizing kinematic information from children particles, particularly those from top quark decays.

listitem.para:
__cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent.
Initializes self.root_leaves to define specific ROOT branches (res_pt, spec_pt, res_decay_pt, top_perm_pt, etc.) and associates them with the appropriate helper functions (res, spc, rdcy, sdcy, tpm) for data processing during InterpretROOT.
Initializes several internal Python dictionaries (res_kinematics, spec_kinematics, res_pdgid_kinematics, spec_pdgid_kinematics, res_decay_mode, spec_decay_mode, fractional, dr_clustering, top_children_dr) for structured storage of processed kinematic data.
Initializes a new C++ childrenkinematics object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr. It then upcasts self.ptr to a childrenkinematics* and assigns it to self.tt for specialized access.
__dealloc__(self): Deallocates the C++ childrenkinematics object pointed to by self.tt, ensuring proper memory management.

Postprocessing(self): This method is called after all data has been interpreted from ROOT files. It performs extensive organization and structuring of the raw kinematic data collected by the helper functions into meaningful Python dictionaries.
Kinematics Organization: Populates res_kinematics and spec_kinematics for primary kinematic variables (pt, eta, phi, energy).
PDG ID-based Kinematics: Processes PDG IDs to group kinematics by particle type, populating res_pdgid_kinematics and spec_pdgid_kinematics.
Decay Mode Analysis: Organizes decay-related kinematics (is_lepton, mass, dR) into res_decay_mode and spec_decay_mode, categorizing by leptonic/hadronic decay.
Fractional Kinematics: Calculates and stores fractional pT and energy contributions for different particle types, populating fractional.
Delta R Clustering: Processes dR values from top permutations and decay children, populating dr_clustering and top_children_dr.
Clears intermediate r_data, s_data, r_decay, s_decay, and top_pem storage.
cdef void transform_dict_keys(self): This method is a placeholder from SelectionTemplate and is intentionally left empty in this implementation.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The ChildrenKinematics class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class ChildrenKinematics: Declares ChildrenKinematics as a Cython extension type.
cdef childrenkinematics *tt: A C++ pointer to a childrenkinematics object, which is the specialized C++ backend for children kinematics processing.
self.ptr = new childrenkinematics(): Initializes the base SelectionTemplate's C++ pointer.
self.tt = <childrenkinematics*>self.ptr: This line demonstrates C++ upcasting, allowing ChildrenKinematics to fulfill the SelectionTemplate interface while internally managing its specific childrenkinematics data.
from AnalysisG.core.selection_template cimport SelectionTemplate: Imports the base SelectionTemplate class, from which ChildrenKinematics inherits.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports utility functions for string encoding/decoding and data structures.
